meta.setting: spring cave beside a worked mining gulch in Jefferson country
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in hidden water and cave danger

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining, travel, settlement, and water pressure in the Whiskeytown region.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Respectful regional grounding for Native knowledge of local waterways.

image_prompt.subject: a hidden spring cave with cold seep water, mineral-stained stone, bucket rope, lamp smoke, horse tracks outside, and two travelers crouched near the entrance
image_prompt.scene: late afternoon at a rocky gulch mouth; a Native woman points toward the safer footing while a miner lowers a tin bucket, no readable signs or labels
image_prompt.composition: square cave-mouth scene with dark spring water in foreground, figures at the threshold, and dry road country visible beyond
image_prompt.camera: low in-engine exploration view from just inside the cave, close to wet stone and bucket rope
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the water look valuable, cold, and hard to reach without turning the cave into a shrine.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine hidden-water scene
render_traits: slightly simplified travelers, cave mouth, bucket, rope, tracks, and water surface
modest texture resolution on wet stone, mineral crust, tin, rope, cloth, mud, and dust
hard ambient occlusion under rock lip, figures, bucket, horse tracks, and cave ledges
cool cave shadow against hot late-day exterior light
believable 1899 clothing, water gear, lamp soot, and trail wear
playable threshold composition with water access, hiding space, and danger underfoot
style_modifiers.palette: cold spring blue, mineral white, wet stone gray, rope tan, tin dull silver, lamp black, dry dust ochre, and late sun gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, markings, papers, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
caricatured Native clothing or invented symbols

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The spring cave, cold water, mineral stains, bucket rope, travelers, tracks, and dry exterior are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to rugged 1899 water access and trail life.
The image treats Native knowledge respectfully and keeps the cave practical, not mystical.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
